name: naive-clean
delta: the rawest depth-first backtracker: row-major, no heuristics, no breaks

name: naive-codegen
delta: same algorithm, a 16×16-specialised unrolled hot loop

name: rowmajor
delta: the row-major control (same as NAIVE-CLEAN, named for the path study)

name: rowmajor-bottomup
delta: fill bottom-to-top instead of top-to-bottom (Blackwood's scan direction)

name: spiral-in
delta: fill the outer ring inward instead of row-major (the cloister spiral)

name: spiral-out
delta: spiral from the centre outward instead of inward

name: border-first
delta: fill the whole border ring first, then the interior

name: verhaard-comb
delta: a horizontal band then vertical teeth (Verhaard's COMB order)

name: border-mrv
delta: choose the most-constrained empty cell dynamically (MRV) instead of a fixed order

name: mrv-rare
delta: try pieces carrying globally-rare colours first (Selby/Riordan rarity)

name: mrv-fc
delta: add forward-checking: reject a placement that empties any neighbour's domain

name: mrv-ac3
delta: extend the look-ahead to arc-consistency (AC-3) over the frontier

name: mrv-gacolor
delta: add Régin per-colour all-different reasoning on the remaining supply

name: break-1
delta: allow ≤1 broken edge per cell on a depth schedule (Blackwood's ladder)

name: break-2
delta: allow up to 2 broken edges at one cell (double-breaks the community 460s use)

name: verhaard-slip
delta: Verhaard's interior edge-slip schedule instead of Blackwood's ladder

name: blackwood-comb-break
delta: run the Blackwood break ladder on Verhaard's COMB fill order

name: mcgavin-c
delta: the community's own record engine (run as published, cited not re-run here)
note: the fastest DFS the community has measured (~295M nodes/s): a codegen backtracker with Blackwood's break scheme. Runs on its own puzzle build, not our variants.

name: blackwood-cs
delta: the community's own record engine (run as published, cited not re-run here)
note: Joshua Blackwood's own C# record engine. Public, builds on .NET, but hardcodes its 256 pieces and thread count, so it runs the plain puzzle, not our corner-pinned grid.
